prose:
Method provenance block – Propane internal-rotation barriers
Property: Torsional (internal-rotation) barrier heights per scanned C–C dihedral (energy difference between highest and lowest relaxed‐scan point)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding, no Gaussian basis)
Geometry provenance: starting coordinates taken from user-supplied propane.xyz; for each of the two unique H–C–C–C and C–C–C–H dihedrals, a 24-point relaxed internal-coordinate scan was carried out, each point fully optimized with ASE BFGS (fmax ≤ 0.05 eV Å⁻¹) at the same GFN2-xTB level (i.e. GFN2-xTB//GFN2-xTB).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model invoked).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb 6 (via chemkit conformational-analysis skill, scan subcommand; see cli_invocation below).
Convergence & caveats: all 24 points of both dihedral scans converged (`n_converged = 24`); integrity check reports status "ok" and `trustworthy: true`; no warnings were returned. Density-fitting is intrinsic to xTB; no fallbacks occurred.

Headline rotation barriers (interpolated values, kcal mol⁻¹):
 • Dihedral H4–C1–C2–C3: 2.6636595 kcal mol⁻¹
 • Dihedral C1–C2–C3–H9: 2.6650949 kcal mol⁻¹
(The grid-point maxima give 2.6753 and 2.6758 kcal mol⁻¹, respectively; values differ by <0.02 kcal mol⁻¹.)  The value reported above is the slightly higher of the two interpolated barriers.

Live log: calculation ran with live output to conformational-analysis_20260625-115725.out (tailable during execution).  Key artifacts (relaxed scan trajectories and energy plots) were written to the paths listed in the result JSON (e.g. …dih4_1_2_3.xyz/.png, …dih1_2_3_9.xyz/.png).  CLI command recorded in the JSON: `scan --method xtb propane.xyz --out …/live.json`.

These barriers, ~2.66 kcal mol⁻¹, are typical for ethane-like single C–C bond rotation at the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical level; they represent single-conformer, gas-phase estimates without vibrational or entropic corrections.
